Results.Title: Web Browser XSS Protection Not Enabled
Results.Description: The Anti-XSS protection header (X-XSS-Protection) is not set or is disabled. This allows reflected XSS attacks to execute in the browser.
Results.Remediation: Set the response header X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block in the application middleware or reverse proxy configuration.

Results.Title: X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
Results.Description: The Anti-MIME-Sniffing header X-Content-Type-Options is not set to 'nosniff', which allows MIME type confusion attacks.
Results.Remediation: Set the response header X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff in the application middleware.

Results.Title: Cross-Site Scripting (Reflected)
Results.Description: The endpoint /api/search reflects user input in the response body without proper encoding. This allows an attacker to inject malicious JavaScript via the 'q' parameter.
Results.Remediation: Sanitize all user inputs and apply output encoding. Use a Content Security Policy (CSP) header.
